content.full_text:
February 22, 2007

Angie BC DeVoss
Administrative Analyst II
County of San Diego-HHSA
1705 5th Avenue, Rm 318
San Diego, CA 92101

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-07-018

Dear Ms. DeVoss:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the gift provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).1 As we discussed in our telephone conversations, after you filed your request for advice, your human resources department instructed you to fill out the form and state that you have nothing to declare. Because you have not been able to secure a copy of the Conflicts of Interest Code of your agency, and because you believe your employer has told you that you are not in a disclosure category in which you must disclose economic interests on the Statement of Economic Interests (Form 700), you have withdrawn your request. If at a future date, you obtain a copy of the Conflicts of Interest Code, and you are told your disclosure category requires you to disclose your economic interests, and you still have questions, you stated that you will write in for advice again.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Heather M. Rowan
Counsel, Legal Division

1 Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
